title: When Reflecting on Outcomes: - Be Honest: Ask,
subtitle: Own Successes AND Failures
description:
When reflecting on outcomes:
- Be honest: Ask, "What role did I really play in this success or failure?"
- Balance: Acknowledge both personal effort and external factors.
- Learn from mistakes: Use failures as opportunities to grow, not excuses to deflect.

Example: If a project goes well, credit teamwork and planning, not just your input.

faq.question: How do I track my progress with this habit?
faq.answer: Use the check-in method described above and track it in the Life OS app for consistent progress monitoring.

faq.question: What if I miss a day or forget to do this habit?
faq.answer: Don't worry! Life OS habits are designed to be flexible. Just get back on track the next day without judgment.

faq.question: How long does it take to form this habit?
faq.answer: Research shows habits can take 21-66 days to form. Focus on consistency rather than perfection, and use the app to build momentum.

howTo.title: Define the metric baseline
howTo.summary: Identify key performance indicators relevant to your outcome. Establish a clear baseline to measure changes against, ensuring you have quantifiable data to analyze.

howTo.title: Conduct a 10-minute reflection
howTo.summary: Select a recent outcome and spend 10 minutes writing a one-sentence summary. List three observable facts related to the outcome to ground your reflection in evidence.

howTo.title: Categorize contributions
howTo.summary: Divide your contributions into four buckets: personal actions, team/system actions, external context, and counterfactuals. This helps clarify the factors influencing the outcome.

howTo.title: Plan a micro-experiment
howTo.summary: Based on your reflection, design a small experiment to test a hypothesis related to the outcome. Specify actions, metrics, and a timeline for evaluation.

howTo.title: Check in with LifeOS Brali app
howTo.summary: Log your reflections and results in the Brali LifeOS app. Use the link to access the app and ensure you track your progress: https://metalhatscats.com/life-os/honest-outcome-reflection-bias/.
